Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Matter Not Reported.

Michael Patrick Rooney, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Gloria Tran, who was not placed under oath and did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Henry Young, a California real estate broker.
Default was entered by the Court against defendant Henry Young, a California real estate broker, on January 9, 2012
No Doe defendants were named in plaintiff's fourth amended complaint.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Gloria Tran, an individual, and against defendant Henry Young, a California real estate broker, as follows:

Damages:
- Lost equity from the floreclosure sale - $300,000.00.
- Funds put into the put into the project after closing of the loan - $163,380.99.
- Moving costs after the foreclosure - $2,500.00.
- Emotional distress - $100,000.00.
- Broker commission - $6,000.00.
Costs - Per Memorandum of Costs.

Total - $571,880.99.

Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a proposed Judgment to the Court.

Off Calendar Apr-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Motion for preliminary injunction to Stay Foreclosure. Appearances: Michael Patrick Rooney, Attorney at Law, (415) 533-0282, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Ben Hamburg, Esq. of the Hamburg Law Corporation, (510) 985-2600, appearing for Defendant Shirley Cervantes. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff has not shown there is a likelihood that she will prevail on the merits. Insufficient showing that the harm plaintiff will suffer, if the injunction is not issued, will outweigh the harm defendants will suffer, if the injunction is issued. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/xSK)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Ann Mendoza, CSR #6812
